en.extract:
The southern shrikebill (Clytorhynchus pachycephaloides), or brown flycatcher, is a songbird species in the family Monarchidae. It is found in New Caledonia and Vanuatu. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized:

C. p. pachycephaloides - Elliot, 1870: Found on New Caledonia
C. p. grisescens - Sharpe, 1899: Originally described as a separate species. Found on Banks Islands (Vanuatu)


References

The '''southern shrikebill''' ('''''Clytorhynchus pachycephaloides'''''), or '''brown flycatcher''', is a [[songbird]] [[species]] in the [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Monarchidae]]. It is found in [[New Caledonia]] and [[Vanuatu]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

==Subspecies==
Two [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.4|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.4|doi-access=free}}</ref>
* ''C. p. pachycephaloides'' - <small>[[Daniel Giraud Elliot|Elliot]], 1870</small>: Found on New Caledonia
* ''C. p. grisescens'' - <small>[[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1899</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found on [[Banks Islands]] (Vanuatu)
